title: Calculation of Interior Lighting Power Allowance Using the Building
full_text:
4.2.1.5 Calculation of Interior Lighting Power Allowance Using the Building
Calculation of Interior Lighting Power Allowance Using the Building
Area Method
(See Note A-4.2.1.5.)
1) Calculation of interior lighting power allowance using the building area method
as provided in this Article may be applied only where
  a) the interior lighting power allowance is being determined for an entire building,
and
  b) the building type or an equivalent building type is identified in Table 4.2.1.5.
2) Except as provided in Sentence (3), interior lighting power allowance calculations
shall be based on the primary use for which the building is intended.
3) Where 10% or more of the gross lighted area of the building can be classified
as being of a building type other than the principal type for the building, the interior
lighting power allowance shall be calculated using the space-by-space method described
in Article 4.2.1.6.
4) Where the building is of a type other than those listed in Table 4.2.1.5. and no
equivalent building type can be selected, the interior lighting power allowance shall be
calculated using the space-by-space method described in Article 4.2.1.6.
5) The interior lighting power allowance using the building area method shall be
calculated by multiplying the lighting power density, in W/m2, for the building type
provided in Table 4.2.1.5. by the gross lighted area, in m2.
Table 4.2.1.5.

sentences.text: Calculation of interior lighting power allowance using the building area method as provided in this Article may be applied only where

clauses.text: the interior lighting power allowance is being determined for an entire building, and
the building type or an equivalent building type is identified in Table 4.2.1.5.

sentences.text: Except as provided in Sentence (3), interior lighting power allowance calculations shall be based on the primary use for which the building is intended.

Where 10% or more of the gross lighted area of the building can be classified as being of a building type other than the principal type for the building, the interior lighting power allowance shall be calculated using the space-by-space method described in Article 4.2.1.6.

Where the building is of a type other than those listed in Table 4.2.1.5. and no equivalent building type can be selected, the interior lighting power allowance shall be calculated using the space-by-space method described in Article 4.2.1.6.

The interior lighting power allowance using the building area method shall be calculated by multiplying the lighting power density, in W/m2, for the building type provided in Table 4.2.1.5. by the gross lighted area, in m2.
